texts.label: text
texts.orig: Cool Planet Technologies Limited (CPT) and its partner Helmholtz-Zentrum hereon GmbH (Hereon)  in  conjunction  with  Holcim  Deutschland  GmbH  (Holcim),  are  carrying  out  a  one month 'calibration' test programme using Hereon's Carbon Capture Test Stand Unit located at Holcim's Höver cement works.
texts.text: Cool Planet Technologies Limited (CPT) and its partner Helmholtz-Zentrum hereon GmbH (Hereon)  in  conjunction  with  Holcim  Deutschland  GmbH  (Holcim),  are  carrying  out  a  one month 'calibration' test programme using Hereon's Carbon Capture Test Stand Unit located at Holcim's Höver cement works.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This brief report provides a summary of the scope and preliminary results from the first 2weeks of operation.
texts.text: This brief report provides a summary of the scope and preliminary results from the first 2weeks of operation.

texts.orig: 2.0 Description of the Test Stand Unit
texts.text: 2.0 Description of the Test Stand Unit

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Fig. 2.1 shows a simplified PFD of the equipment contained within Hereon's Test Stand Unit (constructed for the METPORE II and MemKoR projects) with the main flow stream, used during the test programme, highlighted.  The unit has been designed to work under different configurations depending on the objectives of the test.
texts.text: Fig. 2.1 shows a simplified PFD of the equipment contained within Hereon's Test Stand Unit (constructed for the METPORE II and MemKoR projects) with the main flow stream, used during the test programme, highlighted.  The unit has been designed to work under different configurations depending on the objectives of the test.

texts.label: page_header
texts.orig: Holcim's Höver Decarbonisation Project - Phase 1 Test Stand Update CPT-P-003 Revision A1
texts.text: Holcim's Höver Decarbonisation Project - Phase 1 Test Stand Update CPT-P-003 Revision A1

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The flue gas temperature, at the Höver plant varies between 110 - 125 o C.  The distance between the flue gas tie-in point of the main stack to the Test Stand Unit is close to 80 metres. At the rates that the unit is operating at (50 - 70 Nm 3 /h) the arrival temperature into the Test Stand is close to ambient, not the 50 o C as shown in Figure 2.1.  Most of the water contained within the flue gas drops our as condensate in the inlet 2-stage separator.  Additional cooling of the gas is provided by the water/gas cooler downstream of the separator.  Small quantities of water have been detected at this point.  Downstream of the cooler is the superheater where 10 o C of superheat is applied to the gas to ensure no water condenses within the membrane unit. Preventing any potential damage to the membrane due to the combination of NOx and SOx with free water.  Downstream of the superheater is a duplex filter designed to remove any particulates not removed in the feed separator and cooler.
texts.text: The flue gas temperature, at the Höver plant varies between 110 - 125 o C.  The distance between the flue gas tie-in point of the main stack to the Test Stand Unit is close to 80 metres. At the rates that the unit is operating at (50 - 70 Nm 3 /h) the arrival temperature into the Test Stand is close to ambient, not the 50 o C as shown in Figure 2.1.  Most of the water contained within the flue gas drops our as condensate in the inlet 2-stage separator.  Additional cooling of the gas is provided by the water/gas cooler downstream of the separator.  Small quantities of water have been detected at this point.  Downstream of the cooler is the superheater where 10 o C of superheat is applied to the gas to ensure no water condenses within the membrane unit. Preventing any potential damage to the membrane due to the combination of NOx and SOx with free water.  Downstream of the superheater is a duplex filter designed to remove any particulates not removed in the feed separator and cooler.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The driving force for the membrane unit is the Liquid Ring Compressor pump on the permeate stream.  The suction pressure of the pump can be varied between 0.05 - 0.15 bara.  Combined with an inlet pressure of just above atmospheric the vacuum pump allows pressure ratios of between 6.66 - 20 to be tested across the membrane unit.  The discharge from the pump is tied back into the return line to the main flue gas stack.
texts.text: The driving force for the membrane unit is the Liquid Ring Compressor pump on the permeate stream.  The suction pressure of the pump can be varied between 0.05 - 0.15 bara.  Combined with an inlet pressure of just above atmospheric the vacuum pump allows pressure ratios of between 6.66 - 20 to be tested across the membrane unit.  The discharge from the pump is tied back into the return line to the main flue gas stack.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A blower is provided on the retentate side of the membrane module to overcome the back pressure in the return line to the main flue gas stack.
texts.text: A blower is provided on the retentate side of the membrane module to overcome the back pressure in the return line to the main flue gas stack.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The unit has been modified to include the new PolyActive™ rectangular membrane module designed to test the performance of the membrane in this new configuration.  Figures 2.5, 2.6 and 2.7 provide; Technical Drawing of the unit,  photos of the new module at Hereon's offices and in-situ within the Test Stand Unit.
texts.text: The unit has been modified to include the new PolyActive™ rectangular membrane module designed to test the performance of the membrane in this new configuration.  Figures 2.5, 2.6 and 2.7 provide; Technical Drawing of the unit,  photos of the new module at Hereon's offices and in-situ within the Test Stand Unit.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The objectives of the test programme are to calibrate Hereon's existing simulation model with flue  gas  from  a  cement  plant  and  to  determine  the  performance  of  the  new  rectangular membrane module design over the existing circular stack design.
texts.text: The objectives of the test programme are to calibrate Hereon's existing simulation model with flue  gas  from  a  cement  plant  and  to  determine  the  performance  of  the  new  rectangular membrane module design over the existing circular stack design.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The  test  programme  has  been  designed  by  Hereon  to  consider  a  range  of  feed  flowrates, temperatures, and vacuum pump pressures (pressure ratio).  Table 3.1 provides a summary of the proposed Range of Conditions Matrix.
texts.text: The  test  programme  has  been  designed  by  Hereon  to  consider  a  range  of  feed  flowrates, temperatures, and vacuum pump pressures (pressure ratio).  Table 3.1 provides a summary of the proposed Range of Conditions Matrix.

texts.label: caption
texts.orig: Figure 3.1 Range of Test Conditions Matrix
texts.text: Figure 3.1 Range of Test Conditions Matrix

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Table 3.2 provides a summary of the test points taken to date and the plans for w/c 18 th April. Those days shaded grey denote periods where the unit was switched into 'air mode'.  These were periods where the unit was left running with outside air, bypassing the flue gas, via a diverter valve.
texts.text: Table 3.2 provides a summary of the test points taken to date and the plans for w/c 18 th April. Those days shaded grey denote periods where the unit was switched into 'air mode'.  These were periods where the unit was left running with outside air, bypassing the flue gas, via a diverter valve.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Since the unit has been on site Holcim has been 'promoting' its successful operation internally and externally and has requested that the unit remain on site until the first week in May to allow their  Head  of  Decarbonisation  to  see  the  unit  in  operation.    CPT  and  hereon  will  be  taking advantage of this by adding additional test points and changing the operation (slightly); testing the membrane at lower operating temperatures and trying out a modified filter unit containing desiccant to test the performance of a dryer in place of a superheater.
texts.text: Since the unit has been on site Holcim has been 'promoting' its successful operation internally and externally and has requested that the unit remain on site until the first week in May to allow their  Head  of  Decarbonisation  to  see  the  unit  in  operation.    CPT  and  hereon  will  be  taking advantage of this by adding additional test points and changing the operation (slightly); testing the membrane at lower operating temperatures and trying out a modified filter unit containing desiccant to test the performance of a dryer in place of a superheater.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Hereon provides daily reports taking data directly from the SCADA system, built within the Test Stand Unit.  The results are transmitted to all parties via a web portal.  Every few days Hereon runs a comparative model between experimental results and those predicted by their simulation model.
texts.text: Hereon provides daily reports taking data directly from the SCADA system, built within the Test Stand Unit.  The results are transmitted to all parties via a web portal.  Every few days Hereon runs a comparative model between experimental results and those predicted by their simulation model.

texts.label: caption
texts.orig: Figure 4.1 provides a graphical representation of the preliminary results and shows a very close correlation between experimental and prediction.
texts.text: Figure 4.1 provides a graphical representation of the preliminary results and shows a very close correlation between experimental and prediction.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: On completion of the Test Programme Hereon will be providing a detailed test report and an updated software model that CPT will be using in their cycle optimisation study.
texts.text: On completion of the Test Programme Hereon will be providing a detailed test report and an updated software model that CPT will be using in their cycle optimisation study.
